messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following peer-to-peer lending platform privacy policy for GDPR compliance:

"LendPeer connects borrowers and investors. We collect your name, national ID, income details, credit history, and bank account information to assess loan applications and facilitate transactions. Processing is based on contract performance and legal obligations under financial services regulation. Automated credit scoring is used to determine loan eligibility. You have the right to request human review of any automated credit decision. Data is shared with credit bureaus and our banking partner (Clearbank, UK). Records are kept for 7 years. Our DPO is at dpo@lendpeer.com. We notify the FCA and ICO within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Contract performance (Art. 6(1)(b)) and legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) both stated and appropriate for regulated financial services.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] National ID and financial distress indicators can imply protected characteristics. No acknowledgement of this risk or safeguard is included.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Loan assessment, transaction facilitation, and regulatory compliance are clear and separate purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and proportionate for peer-to-peer lending underwriting.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 7-year retention stated and grounded in financial services regulation.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Right to human review of automated decisions (Art. 22) stated. Other rights not individually listed.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PASS] Credit bureaus and Clearbank named as recipients.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PARTIAL] Clearbank is UK-based. Credit bureaus may operate internationally — not addressed.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [PASS] DPO at dpo@lendpeer.com disclosed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour notification to FCA and ICO stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (national ID and financial distress risk not addressed), Art. 15-21 (most rights not individually listed), Art. 46 (credit bureau international operations not addressed)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Acknowledge that national ID and credit data may imply protected characteristics and state safeguards
- Add full data subject rights list with contact instructions
- Address international operations of credit bureaus and applicable transfer safeguards
